project_overview.project_name: Emergency Social Safety Net Project for Crisis and COVID-19 Response in Lebanon - Additional Financing
project_overview.project_development_objective: (a) Provide cash transfers and social services to Lebanese population suffering from extreme poverty and need affected by economic crisis and COVID-19; (b) In case of crisis or emergency, ensure immediate and effective response to such emergencies or crises
project_overview.total_financing: $4 million USD (additional financing); Total project financing: $250 million USD
project_overview.implementation_period: May 2022 to August 2022 (additional financing); Original project closure: February 29, 2024
project_overview.beneficiaries: Approximately 147,000 families (762,952 individuals) targeted for basic income support; 2,700 additional families (12,300 individuals) from additional financing; focus on extremely poor households

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - Lebanon faces significant political instability, weak institutions, regional security threats, and economic crisis; affected by Syrian refugee crisis and regional conflicts

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Basic Income Support Cash Transfers
key_components.description: Provide monthly cash transfers of $20 per person (up to 6 family members) for food needs plus $25 fixed amount per family for non-food basic needs to extremely poor households
Supplementary cash transfers ($200-300 per eligible child annually) to students aged 13-18 at risk of school dropout to maintain human capital
Improve access to social services for vulnerable populations through service packages

key_components.component_name: Component 4: Social Safety Net System Strengthening
key_components.description: Build sustainable national social registry (DAEM platform), strengthen institutional capacity, monitoring and evaluation, grievance mechanisms
Reserve for rapid response to future crises or emergencies

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Prime Minister's Office (Presidency of Council of Ministers) through Central Management Unit (CMU); Ministry of Social Affairs; Ministry of Education and Higher Education
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Inter-ministerial coordination committee; Central Management Unit responsible for overall project management; World Food Programme (WFP) contracted for cash transfer implementation and beneficiary verification; Central Inspection (CIO) oversees IMPACT platform and data protection; Independent monitoring agency for verification and compliance
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: Streamlined competitive procedures with shortened bidding timelines; procurement from UN agencies where permitted; increased thresholds for quotation requests; Central Management Unit has decision-making authority; systematic tracking of procurement transactions
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Independent monitoring agency conducts monthly data collection and quarterly reporting; WFP conducts post-distribution monitoring; quarterly beneficiary satisfaction surveys; real-time monitoring through IMPACT platform; external audit by independent private auditor; grievance redress mechanism with 14-day response target

identified_risks.risk_description: Political instability, weak institutions, government formation challenges, parliamentary elections (May 2022), presidential elections (October 2022), regional security threats affecting Lebanon's political and economic stability
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Continuous monitoring and screening measures throughout implementation; dialogue with government on policy reforms; support for institutional strengthening

identified_risks.risk_description: Severe financial crisis, capital flight, currency collapse, multiple exchange rates, banking system failures, extreme inflation (85% in 2020, 157% Jan-Jan 2021-2022), food price inflation (328% Jan 2021-Jan 2022), deep recession with 58.1% GDP contraction
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Cash transfers denominated in USD to preserve purchasing power; IMF staff-level agreement signed March 2022 for potential macroeconomic stabilization; World Bank public finance review support; temporary measure to mitigate currency and inflation risks

identified_risks.risk_description: Government inability to close $12 million financing gap; absence of comprehensive social protection strategy framework; expansion of social safety nets without overarching policy framework
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Ongoing policy dialogue with government; support for development of 'Lebanon Social Protection Strategy Framework'; coordination with other development partners; technical assistance as needed

identified_risks.risk_description: Transition from food vouchers (8-year track record) to cash transfers through money transfer agents; newly launched DAEM registration platform (December 2021) with potential functionality risks; system integration challenges
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Leverage accumulated experience from current implementation; proven effectiveness of cash transfers through money transfer agents; successful experience of other partners implementing cash transfers in Lebanon; high-level technical expertise from private sector and World Bank systems specialists

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited institutional capacity for rapid project implementation; staff turnover and salary delays for social workers; limited capacity in grievance mechanisms, M&E functions; need for full-time staff dedication
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project funds additional specialist positions; contract with WFP for beneficiary verification; dedicated staff assignments; specialized units in social protection information system; capacity building and training programs

identified_risks.risk_description: High-risk environment for cash transfers requiring enhanced controls; potential delays in fund flows through Treasury account; limited internal control systems; risks of inaccurate beneficiary lists and payments to ineligible beneficiaries; exchange rate volatility; limited audit capacity of Court of Accounts
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Dedicated financial management officer; updated project operations manual; Excel-based transaction recording and reporting; independent monitoring agency for periodic verification; payments in USD to mitigate currency risk; independent external auditor contracted; WFP manages cash transfer operations; quarterly financial reporting; dedicated project account at Central Bank

identified_risks.risk_description: Perception of corruption in fragile political environment; delays in procurement processing due to slow decision-making; coordination complexity with multiple ministries; capacity constraints
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Continued services from experienced Central Management Unit; procurement decisions concentrated in CMU; good record-keeping practices; independent monitoring agency for field visits and physical verification; excellent communication strategy with civil society

identified_risks.risk_description: Risk of exclusion of vulnerable groups (smaller families, near-poor populations) due to targeting mechanism characteristics; potential gender-based violence and sexual exploitation/abuse risks; social tensions from beneficiary selection
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Social impact assessment identifying risks and mitigation measures; labor management procedures; grievance redress mechanism with referral pathways for GBV/SEA cases; stakeholder consultation and engagement plan; awareness campaigns; strong grievance mechanism with 14-day response target; prevention and response plan for SEA/GBV

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential objections from applicants deemed ineligible; community resistance to beneficiary selection; stakeholder concerns about program design and implementation
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strong grievance and complaint mechanism; awareness and communication campaigns; stakeholder consultations during project design; ongoing stakeholder engagement as part of environmental and social commitment plan

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS2: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
ESS5: Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement
ESS6: Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources
ESS7: Indigenous Peoples/Sub-Saharan African Historically Underserved Traditional Local Communities
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Multi-level grievance mechanism: (1) Project-level grievance mechanism through IMPACT platform with active call center (operational since December 2021); (2) Complaint and grievance processing unit within Ministry of Social Affairs; (3) World Bank Grievance Redress Service; (4) World Bank Inspection Panel. Response target: 14 days for grievance resolution. Referral pathways established for GBV/SEA cases.
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Target indicators include: proportion of female-headed households (baseline 13.2%, target 12%); girls' school enrollment and attendance (target 95%); women as beneficiaries of social services (target 60%); gender-based violence victims referred to services (target 100 cases); prevention and response plan for sexual exploitation and abuse/sexual harassment
vulnerable_groups: Extremely poor households (below extreme poverty line)
Children under 18 years (76% of beneficiary households)
School-age children at risk of dropout (ages 13-18)
Near-poor populations at risk of falling into poverty

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project designed to address immediate humanitarian needs during economic and health crisis; cash transfers help prevent further deterioration and social unrest; social safety net strengthens resilience; grievance mechanisms reduce tensions; however, project operates in highly fragile context with limited ability to address underlying conflict drivers; project focuses on immediate poverty alleviation rather than conflict resolution
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Lebanon faces multiple overlapping crises: (1) Severe economic and financial crisis since 2019 with 58.1% GDP contraction; (2) COVID-19 pandemic impacts; (3) Beirut port explosion (August 2020) with massive economic consequences; (4) Extreme inflation (food prices up 483% by January 2022); (5) Poverty rates exceeding 50% of population; (6) Weak state institutions; (7) Regional instability and Syrian refugee crisis (1.5+ million refugees); (8) Political instability with government formation challenges; (9) Banking system collapse and currency crisis; (10) Humanitarian emergency with 46% of households unable to meet basic needs
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Project operates in context of regional security threats affecting Lebanon; political instability and weak governance create implementation risks; security situation affects beneficiary access and staff safety; project does not directly address security issues but aims to reduce social tensions through poverty alleviation; cash transfers help prevent desperation-driven crime and unrest
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: Yes - Lebanon hosts 1.5+ million Syrian refugees; project benefits both Lebanese nationals and refugee populations; LSCTF (Lebanon Crisis Trust Fund) specifically established to support Lebanese host communities affected by Syrian refugee crisis; additional financing sourced from LSCTF; project addresses needs of vulnerable populations including refugees and displaced persons; targeting mechanism includes both Lebanese citizens and eligible refugee populations
